title: SCA Exemption Usage Tracking (RTS on SCA & CSC)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'PSD2 RTS on SCA & CSC' → 'Art.97'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PSD2 Art.97 (Strong customer authentication) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.22.2: SCA Exemption Usage Tracking (RTS on SCA & CSC).
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.22.2' running on index=payments sourcetype=psd2:sca — aspsp_id, psu_id, challenge, sca_exemption, outcome, authentication_method, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

dataSources: `index=payments` `sourcetype=psd2:sca` — aspsp_id, psu_id, challenge, sca_exemption, outcome, authentication_method
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=payments sourcetype="psd2:sca" earliest=-24h
| eval sca_dim="exemption"
| eval challenge_flag=if(challenge="true" OR challenge="1",1,0)
| stats count as authn, sum(eval(outcome="success")) as ok, sum(challenge_flag) as ch, dc(psu_id) as psu by aspsp_id, sca_dim
| lookup psd2_sca_baseline.csv aspsp_id OUTPUT challenge_rate_baseline AS baseline
| eval challenge_rate=round(100*ch/authn,3)
| eval delta=round(challenge_rate-baseline,3)
| eval uc_id="22.22.2"
| where authn>100 AND (delta>2 OR delta<-2)
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, sca_dim, authn, challenge_rate, baseline, delta
description: Monitors PSD2 strong customer authentication telemetry against documented baselines to evidence RTS compliance and proportionate exemption usage.
value: Monitors PSD2 strong customer authentication telemetry against documented baselines to evidence RTS compliance and proportionate exemption usage.
implementation: (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
visualization: Time chart (challenge_rate), Bar chart (delta by aspsp_id), Table (top outliers)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count

grandmaExplanation: We keep an eye on PSD2 strong customer authentication telemetry against documented baselines to evidence RTS compliance and proportionate exemption usage — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=payments` `sourcetype=psd2:sca` — aspsp_id, psu_id, challenge, sca_exemption, outcome, authentication_method.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="psd2:sca" earliest=-24h
| eval sca_dim="exemption"
| eval challenge_flag=if(challenge="true" OR challenge="1",1,0)
| stats count as authn, sum(eval(outcome="success")) as ok, sum(challenge_flag) as ch, dc(psu_id) as psu by aspsp_id, sca_dim
| lookup psd2_sca_baseline.csv aspsp_id OUTPUT challenge_rate_baseline AS baseline
| eval challenge_rate=round(100*ch/authn,3)
| eval delta=round(challenge_rate-baseline,3)
| eval uc_id="22.22.2"
| where authn>100 AND (delta>2 OR delta<-2)
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, sca_dim, authn, challenge_rate, baseline, delta
```

Understanding this SPL

**SCA Exemption Usage Tracking (RTS on SCA & CSC) (PSD2 RTS on SCA & CSC)** — Monitors PSD2 strong customer authentication telemetry against documented baselines to evidence RTS compliance and proportionate exemption usage.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=payments` `sourcetype=psd2:sca` — aspsp_id, psu_id, challenge, sca_exemption, outcome, authentication_method. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: psd2:sca. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="psd2:sca", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sca_dim** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **challenge_flag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by aspsp_id, sca_dim** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **challenge_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **delta** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_id** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where authn>100 AND (delta>2 OR delta<-2)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **SCA Exemption Usage Tracking (RTS on SCA & CSC) (PSD2 RTS on SCA & CSC)**): table uc_id, aspsp_id, sca_dim, authn, challenge_rate, baseline, delta

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**SCA Exemption Usage Tracking (RTS on SCA & CSC) (PSD2 RTS on SCA & CSC)** — Monitors PSD2 strong customer authentication telemetry against documented baselines to evidence RTS compliance and proportionate exemption usage.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=payments` `sourcetype=psd2:sca` — aspsp_id, psu_id, challenge, sca_exemption, outcome, authentication_method. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart (challenge_rate), Bar chart (delta by aspsp_id), Table (top outliers)
